question: Which department does Ms. Brant belong to?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/empl-Karen.Brant%40company.org> pv:memberOf ?result .
  ?result a pv:Department .
}

question: Could you tell me Sabrina's email address from the Marketing department?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?department pv:name "Marketing" .
  ?person pv:memberOf ?department .
  ?person pv:email ?result .
  ?person pv:name ?name .
  FILTER (CONTAINS(?name, "Sabrina"))
}
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
SELECT ?employee ?name ?email WHERE {
  ?employee foaf:name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), "sabrina"))
  OPTIONAL {?employee pv:email ?email}
} LIMIT 5

PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>

question: How many different Sensor Switches do we offer?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT (COUNT(?resultR) as ?result)
WHERE
{
  ?resultR pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Sensor> .
  ?resultR pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Switch> .
}

question: How many of our suppliers are based in France?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT ?supplier) as ?result)
WHERE
{
  ?product pv:hasSupplier ?supplier .
  ?supplier pv:addressCountry "France" .
}

question: Which supplier in France provides compensators?

SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?product pv:hasSupplier ?result .
  ?product pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Compensator> .

question: Which encoder is the most affordable from a French or German supplier?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?result pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Encoder> .
  ?result pv:price ?priceR .
  ?priceR pv:amount ?price .
  ?result pv:hasSupplier ?supplier .
  ?supplier pv:addressCountry ?country .
  FILTER ( (?country = "France") ||  (?country = "Germany") )
}
ORDER BY ASC(?price)
LIMIT 1
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
SELECT ?encoder ?price ?supplier ?country WHERE {
  ?encoder a pv:Product .
  ?encoder pv:hasCategory ?category .
  ?category rdfs:label ?categoryLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?categoryLabel)), "encoder"))
  ?encoder pv:price ?price .
  ?encoder pv:hasSupplier ?supplier .
  ?supplier pv:country ?country .
  FILTER(?country IN (dbr:France, dbr:Germany))
} ORDER BY ASC(?price) LIMIT 1

SELECT ?service ?price WHERE {?service a pv:Service ; pv:price ?price . } ORDER BY DESC(?price) LIMIT 1

question: Who oversees the most expensive service we offer?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?service a pv:Service .
  ?service pv:hasProductManager ?result .
  ?service pv:price ?priceR .
  ?priceR pv:amount ?price .
}
ORDER BY DESC(?price)
LIMIT 1

question: Which coil is the heaviest while still fitting within a 15mm x 15mm base area?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?result pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Coil> .
  ?result pv:weight_g ?weight_g .
  ?result pv:width_mm ?width_mm .
  ?result pv:depth_mm ?depth_mm .
  FILTER (?width_mm <= 15)
  FILTER (?depth_mm <= 15)
}
ORDER BY DESC(?weight_g)
LIMIT 1

SELECT ?coil ?weight ?width ?length WHERE {
  ?coil a pv:Product .
  ?coil rdfs:label ?label .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?label), "coil"))
  ?coil pv:weight ?weight .
  ?coil pv:width ?width .
  ?coil pv:length ?length .
  FILTER(?width <= 15 && ?length <= 15)
} ORDER BY DESC(?weight) LIMIT 1

question: Which potentiometer has the smallest volume?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?result pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Potentiometer> .
  ?result pv:width_mm ?width_mm .
  ?result pv:depth_mm ?depth_mm .
  ?result pv:height_mm ?height_mm .
  BIND(?width_mm * ?depth_mm * ?height_mm as ?volume)
}
ORDER BY ASC(?volume)
LIMIT 1

question: Which coil has the greatest density?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?result pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Coil> .
  ?result pv:width_mm ?width_mm .
  ?result pv:depth_mm ?depth_mm .
  ?result pv:height_mm ?height_mm .
  ?result pv:weight_g ?weight_g .
  BIND(?width_mm * ?depth_mm * ?height_mm / 1000000000 AS ?volume_m3)
  BIND(?weight_g / 1000 AS ?weight_kg)
  BIND(?weight_kg / ?volume_m3 AS ?density)
}
ORDER BY DESC(?density)
LIMIT 1

  ?product pv:hasSupplier ?supplier .
  ?supplier pv:country <http://dbpedia.org/resource/United_States> .
  ?supplier pv:addressLocality ?result .
}

question: Could you provide a staff phone directory that includes the name, email, and phone number of every employee who doesn’t manage anyone, sorted alphabetically by name?

PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?employee ?name ?email ?phone WHERE {
  ?employee a pv:Employee .
  OPTIONAL { ?employee foaf:name ?name. }
  OPTIONAL { ?employee pv:email ?email. }
  OPTIONAL { ?employee pv:phone ?phone. }
  FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?employee pv:hasDirectReport ?directReport. }
} ORDER BY ?name

question: Is there a service that can handle a BOM containing parts sourced from Russia?
golden:
PREFIX prodi: <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>

ASK
WHERE {
  { # all BOMs with parts from russia
    SELECT ?bom
    WHERE {
      ?bom pv:hasBomPart ?bomPart .
      ?bomPart pv:hasPart ?hw .
      ?hw pv:hasSupplier ?suppl .
      ?suppl pv:addressCountry ?country .
      FILTER (CONTAINS(LCASE(?country), LCASE("Russia")))
    }
  }

  # services eligible for parts associated with these BOMs?
  ?s a pv:Service .
  ?s pv:eligibleFor ?hw .
  ?bom pv:hasBomPart ?bomPart .
  ?bomPart pv:hasPart ?hw .
}
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?service WHERE {
  ?service a pv:Service .
  ?service pv:eligibleFor ?product .
  ?product pv:hasCategory ?category .
  ?category pv:hasBom ?bom .
  ?bom pv:hasBomPart ?bomPart .
  ?bomPart pv:hasSupplier ?supplier .
  ?supplier pv:country ?country .
  FILTER(REGEX(STR(?country), "Russia", "i"))
} LIMIT 10

question: Which suppliers—including their name, country, and price—provide the hardware items that rank from the 6th to the 10th most expensive?
Which departments have more than five employees, and could you list each department's name together with its employee count?
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?department ?name (COUNT(?employee) AS ?employeeCount) WHERE {
  ?employee pv:memberOf ?department .
  ?department a pv:Department .
  OPTIONAL {?department rdfs:label ?name}
} GROUP BY ?department ?name
HAVING(COUNT(?employee) > 5)

question: What is the average price in euros of each supplier's products, rounded to a reasonable amount?
generated:

PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>

PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>

PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?supplier (ROUND(AVG(?amount)) AS ?avgPrice) WHERE {
  ?product pv:hasSupplier ?supplier ;
           pv:price ?price .
  ?price pv:priceAmount ?amount ;
         pv:currency "EUR" .
} GROUP BY ?supplier

question: For each product, could you list the other products it's compatible with and show the price differences between them?
Which product area is most relevant, and what are the top three associated skills?
golden:
PREFIX prodi: <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>

SELECT ?cat ?name
WHERE {
  ?hw a pv:Hardware ;
    pv:hasCategory ?cat .
  ?cat pv:name ?name .
}
GROUP BY ?cat ?name
ORDER BY DESC(COUNT(*))
LIMIT 3

  ?employee a pv:Employee .
  ?employee pv:areaOfExpertise <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Compensator> .

  FILTER(?skill != <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Compensator>)
} GROUP BY ?skill
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 3

question: For each bill of materials, show the count of parts and the total material quantity, but display only those BOMs with more than 600 items, sorted in descending order.
golden:
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX prodi: <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>

SELECT ?bom (COUNT(?part) AS ?partCount) (SUM(?qty) AS ?totalQty)
WHERE {
  ?bom a pv:BillOfMaterial ;
    pv:hasBomPart ?part .
  ?part pv:quantity ?qty_ .
  BIND(xsd:int(?qty_) AS ?qty)
}
GROUP BY ?bom
HAVING (SUM(?qty) > 600)
ORDER BY DESC(SUM(?qty))
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?bom (COUNT(?part) AS ?partCount) (SUM(xsd:integer(?quantity)) AS ?totalQuantity) WHERE {
  ?bom a pv:BillOfMaterial .
  ?bom pv:hasBomPart ?bomPart .
  ?bomPart pv:hasPart ?part .
  ?bomPart pv:quantity ?quantity .
} GROUP BY ?bom
HAVING (COUNT(?part) > 5)
ORDER BY DESC(?partCount)
LIMIT 10

question: Could you give me an updated contact list that includes each employee's name, email, phone number, department, and direct manager?
Can you list the dimensions of hardware items that are wider than they are tall and have a depth under 50 mm?
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?hardware ?width ?height ?depth WHERE {
  ?hardware a pv:Product .
  ?hardware pv:hasCategory ?category .
  ?category rdfs:label ?label .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?label)), "hardware"))
  ?hardware pv:width ?width .
  ?hardware pv:height ?height .
  ?hardware pv:depth ?depth .
  FILTER(xsd:decimal(?width) > xsd:decimal(?height)) .
  FILTER(xsd:decimal(?depth) < 50) .
} LIMIT 5

question: Could you list the IDs and names of hardware items that don't have an active product manager?
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?product ?id ?name WHERE {
  ?product rdf:type pv:Product .
  ?product pv:hasCategory ?category .
  ?category rdfs:label ?categoryName .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?categoryName)), "hardware"))
  FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?product pv:hasProductManager ?manager }
  ?product pv:id ?id .
  ?product rdfs:label ?name .
} LIMIT 10

question: What share of each manager's overall team works in the same department as the manager?
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?manager (COUNT(?employee) as ?totalTeam) (COUNT(?employeeSameDept) as ?sameDeptTeam) (COUNT(?employeeSameDept) / COUNT(?employee) as ?shareSameDept) WHERE {
  ?manager a pv:Manager .
  ?employee pv:hasManager ?manager .
  ?manager pv:memberOf ?dept .
  ?employee pv:memberOf ?dept .
  BIND(?employee AS ?employeeSameDept)
} GROUP BY ?manager
LIMIT 10

question: Which bill of materials has the highest average unit cost for its hardware components, and what is that average?
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?bom ?avgUnitCost WHERE {
  {
    SELECT ?bom (AVG(xsd:decimal(?priceValue)) AS ?avgUnitCost) WHERE {
      ?bom a pv:BillOfMaterial .
      ?bom pv:hasBomPart ?bomPart .
      ?bomPart pv:hasPart ?product .
      ?product pv:price ?price .
      ?price pv:price ?priceValue .
    } GROUP BY ?bom
  }
} ORDER BY DESC(?avgUnitCost) LIMIT 1

question: Could you list any product compatibility cycles—instances where product A states it's compatible with product B and product B likewise lists product A as compatible?
generated:
PREFIX dbc: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Category:>
PREFIX dbd: <http://dbpedia.org/datatype/>
PREFIX dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
PREFIX dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>
PREFIX dbr: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/>
PREFIX dbt: <http://dbpedia.org/resource/Template:>
PREFIX dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/rdfa#>
PREFIX rdfdf: <http://www.openlinksw.com/virtrdf-data-formats#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
PREFIX ecc: <https://ns.eccenca.com/>
PREFIX void: <http://rdfs.org/ns/void#>
PREFIX vann: <http://purl.org/vocab/vann/>
SELECT ?productA ?productB WHERE {
  ?productA pv:compatibleProduct ?productB .
  ?productB pv:compatibleProduct ?productA .
  FILTER(?productA != ?productB)
} LIMIT 10

question: Which hardware items fall within the top 10% of widths, and what are their IDs, names, and width measurements?
Which supplier provides the most reliable inductors?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result
WHERE
{
  ?hardware pv:hasCategory <http://ld.company.org/prod-instances/prod-cat-Inductor> .
  ?hardware pv:reliabilityIndex ?reliabilityIndex .
  ?hardware pv:hasSupplier ?result .
}
ORDER BY DESC(?reliabilityIndex)
LIMIT 1

question: Which five suppliers have the highest average reliability across their products?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result (AVG(?reliabilityIndex) AS ?averageReliabilityIndex)
WHERE
{
  ?hardware pv:reliabilityIndex ?reliabilityIndex .
  ?hardware pv:hasSupplier ?result .
}
GROUP BY ?result
ORDER BY DESC(?averageReliabilityIndex)
LIMIT 5

question: Which department is responsible for the most products, and how many products is that?
golden:
PREFIX pv: <http://ld.company.org/prod-vocab/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?result (COUNT(?result) AS ?count)
WHERE
{
  ?result pv:responsibleFor ?product .
}
GROUP BY ?result
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 1
